tone.notes: Dutch business culture values directness but with formal address (u/uw in formal Dutch). Avoid hyperbole and superlatives — they read as untrustworthy. Emails should be shorter and denser than US equivalents. Get to the point quickly but maintain professional distance in early touches. Data and specifics over adjectives.

blackout_periods.name: King's Day (Koningsdag)
blackout_periods.description: April 27 — national holiday, no business activity

blackout_periods.name: Summer Holidays
blackout_periods.description: July–August Dutch school holidays, reduced B2B responsiveness

localization.measurement_notes: Use meters for berth/boat length. Use liters for fuel. Use EUR.

compliance.opt_out_language: Klik hier om u af te melden voor verdere communicatie.
compliance.required_footer: U ontvangt dit bericht omdat u een jachthaven of maritiem bedrijf beheert.
compliance.notes: GDPR opt-out must be one-click. Do not use pre-checked consent boxes. Store consent records.

cultural_notes: The Dutch marina market is concentrated around the IJsselmeer, Frisian lakes, and coastal areas. Many are jachtclubs (yacht clubs) with committee governance — decision cycles are longer. Target the havenmeester (harbor master) or bedrijfsleider (operations manager) rather than ownership committees for faster decisions. The Netherlands has one of the highest recreational boat ownership rates in Europe — 1.3 million registered vessels for 17 million people.
